questions: bullying er shikar hole ki korbo?
What are the effects of bullying?
answers: Bullying is repeated aggressive behavior — physical, verbal, or social — intended to hurt, intimidate, or control another person, especially someone perceived as weaker.
Bullying holo bar bar karo upor aggressive behavior kora — mara, kharap kotha bola, ba socially isolate kora.
If you're being bullied, tell a trusted adult, document what happened, avoid being alone with the bully, and remember it's not your fault. You deserve to be safe.
Bullying can cause anxiety, depression, low self-esteem, academic problems, and even physical health issues. The effects can last well into adulthood.
